name: ai-code-cleanup
description: Remove AI-generated code slop from branches. Use after AI-assisted coding
capabilities: Cleans up redundant and obvious comments
Fixes unnecessary type casts and workarounds
author: Joseph OBrien
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill with no executable code. SKILL.md and refactoring-plan.template.md are markdown documentation files. The static analyzer misidentified template syntax (e.g., {{VARIABLE}}) as security patterns. Shell command examples in documentation (git diff) are illustrative only. No scripts, network calls, file system operations, environment access, or external commands exist in this skill.
valueStatement: AI-assisted coding often introduces defensive bloat, unnecessary comments, and style inconsistencies. This skill identifies and removes these artifacts to produce cleaner, more maintainable code while preserving functionality.
limitations: Does not execute code or run tests automatically
Requires user to review and apply suggested changes
Cannot fix logic errors or architectural issues
Relies on existing project style conventions

useCases.title: Post-AI coding cleanup
useCases.description: Clean up code after AI-assisted sessions before commits or code reviews

useCases.title: Review preparation
useCases.description: Standardize code style and remove bloat before reviewing pull requests

useCases.title: Quality gate enforcement
useCases.description: Maintain consistent code quality standards across AI-assisted contributions

promptTemplates.title: Clean full branch
promptTemplates.scenario: Remove AI slop from entire branch
promptTemplates.prompt: Remove AI slop from this branch. Identify and remove defensive bloat, unnecessary comments, type casts, and style inconsistencies that do not affect functionality.

promptTemplates.title: Target specific files
promptTemplates.prompt: Remove unnecessary comments and defensive code from src/. Focus on AI-generated artifacts while preserving all error handling and functionality.

promptTemplates.title: Prepare for review
promptTemplates.prompt: Clean up the code in this pull request. Remove AI artifacts, standardize style to match the codebase, and eliminate defensive bloat while maintaining behavior.

promptTemplates.title: Quality assessment
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze this code for AI-generated artifacts. List all unnecessary comments, defensive patterns, type workarounds, and style inconsistencies that should be removed.

bestPractices: Review all suggested changes before applying to verify functionality is preserved
Run existing tests after cleanup to confirm no regressions
Make incremental changes and validate each step when cleaning large files
antiPatterns: Removing legitimate error handling along with defensive bloat
Applying cleanup without understanding the original code context
Expecting the skill to fix logic errors or architectural problems

faq.question: Which AI tools is this skill compatible with?
faq.answer: Works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code for any AI-assisted coding workflow.

faq.question: What patterns does this skill identify?
faq.answer: It detects unnecessary comments, defensive bloat, type workarounds, style inconsistencies, and AI tells.

faq.question: How does this integrate with my workflow?
faq.answer: Use after AI coding sessions. The skill analyzes code and suggests edits you apply manually.

faq.question: Is my code data safe?
faq.answer: Yes. This is a documentation-only skill. No code is transmitted externally or stored anywhere.

faq.question: Why is code quality worse after AI coding?
faq.answer: AI tends toward defensive patterns and verbose explanations. This skill removes that over-engineering.

faq.question: How is this different from a linter?
faq.answer: Linters enforce rules. This skill identifies AI-specific patterns that degrade readability and maintainability.
